reason: Both answers correctly prioritize alert hygiene over rotation changes, propose auditing pages for actionability, suggest business-hours routing for non-urgent alerts, and note that six-person weekly rotation is fine once noise drops, implicitly rejecting follow-the-sun as the fix. Neither mentions reducing sprint commitment during on-call weeks. Answer A explicitly calls out alerting on symptoms (user-facing SLOs) rather than causes, which more directly captures the key point about paging only on user-impacting issues. Answer A also frames a cleaner weekly review metric than Answer B's per-shift budget trigger. Otherwise the two are comparable in correctness and structure, with no padding in either.
